message:
style: revert rustfmt-noise import reorders and exclude html_export goldens from UBS

Purely housekeeping cleanup from the concurrent refactor batch:

- `src/html_export/encryption.rs` and `tests/pages_export_golden.rs`
  had their `use` groups reordered to a different-but-equivalent
  canonical order in a prior commit. The project's rustfmt config
  ultimately wants the *other* order (submodules ahead of sibling
  names, prelude-last), so this just swaps them back to the
  established house style:
    * `aead::{Aead, KeyInit}, Aes256Gcm, Nonce`
    * `aead::{rand_core::RngCore, OsRng}`
    * `base64::Engine; use base64::prelude::BASE64_STANDARD`
    * `serde_json::{Value, json}`
  No code-path change; pure ordering.

- `.ubsignore`: add `tests/golden/html_export/*.golden`. The 71/74 KB
  frozen HTML goldens triggered several false-positive UBS findings
  (large base64 blobs, scrubbed placeholder literals, bundled inline
  scripts) — none of which are production code. Next to the existing
  `tests/fixtures/` exclusion, which has the same rationale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ temp_*.rs
 
 # Test fixtures (not production code)
 tests/fixtures/
+tests/golden/html_export/*.golden
 
 # Lock files (auto-generated)
 Cargo.lock
